recap: Fill Venn diagrams starting from the intersection. Union = total in either or both. Intersection = in both. Always verify all entries sum to the total.

Q1.question: In a Venn diagram, the intersection of sets A and B has 4 elements, only A has 7, only B has 5, and outside both is 3. How many elements are in the universal set?

Q2.question: In a group of 50 people: 30 drink tea, 20 drink coffee, 10 drink both. How many drink neither?

Q3.question: In a class of 35: 20 play football (F), 18 play tennis (T), 8 play both. Draw and fill the Venn diagram, then find P(plays only football).

Q4.question: Using the Venn diagram from Q3, find P(F union T) and P(F intersect T).

steps: F union T includes only F, only T, and both: 12 + 10 + 8 = 30.
F intersect T = 8 students. P(F intersect T) = 8/35.
solution.final_answer: P(F union T) = 6/7, P(F intersect T) = 8/35

Q5.question: A survey of 60 students found: 35 study French (F), 28 study Spanish (S), and 12 study both. A student is chosen at random. Find P(studies French but not Spanish), P(studies exactly one language), and P(studies neither).

summary: Complete the intersection (both) value first, then work outward to fill each region.
Check: all regions must sum to the total number of elements.
P(A union B) = total in A or B or both, divided by total elements.
P(A intersect B) = number in the intersection only, divided by total elements.
